# Session Audio Voice Resolution Implementation Plan

> **For Claude:** REQUIRED SUB-SKILL: Use superpowers:executing-plans to implement this plan task-by-task.

**Goal:** Fix silent loss of play buttons in practice sessions. Drop the one-voice-per-session filter; resolve each item's audio to whichever voice was generated by the earliest lesson that introduced the text.

**Architecture:** Introduce a voice-agnostic session audio pipeline alongside the existing voice-keyed lesson pipeline. A new `get_audio_clip_per_text` RPC applies `DISTINCT ON (normalized_text) ... ORDER BY lesson.order_index NULLS LAST, clip.created_at, clip.id`. Practice (`Session.tsx` + exercise components) reads from a new `SessionAudioContext` whose value is a flat `Map<normalized_text, storage_path>`. The lesson reader (`Lesson.tsx`) continues to use the existing `AudioContext` with voice-keyed lookups unchanged — it needs per-speaker voices for dialogue and cannot be flattened.

**Tech Stack:** PostgreSQL RPC (SQL), TypeScript, React 19, Vitest. Applies to `indonesian` schema only.

---

## Background

### Problem

A practice session currently binds to a single voice:

1. `src/pages/Session.tsx:319-333` picks `lessonFilter`'s `primary_voice`, else "the first lesson's voice" from queue items' contexts (Set iteration order — effectively arbitrary).
2. `fetchAudioMap(texts, [oneVoice])` loads clips only for that voice.
3. `resolveAudioUrl(map, text, voiceId)` in `src/services/audioService.ts:25` looks up under that voice; returns `undefined` otherwise.
4. `PlayButton` at `src/components/PlayButton.tsx:48` renders `null` when the URL is undefined — the button silently disappears.

Audio generation (`scripts/generate-exercise-audio.ts:243`) synthesises each lesson's texts in that lesson's `primary_voice`. So a word introduced in Lesson 4 (Algenib) only has audio in Algenib unless some later lesson happens to include that text in its `lesson_sections` content (which generates an additional Algenib-unrelated clip).

### Impact

587 word/phrase items, 6 primary voices in use (Despina, Achird, Algenib, Sulafat, Orus, Gacrux):

| Voice | Items with audio in this voice | % coverage |
|---|---:|---:|
| Despina | 162 | 27.6% |
| Achird | 129 | 22.0% |
| Algenib | 115 | 19.6% |
| Sulafat | 80 | 13.6% |
| Orus | 58 | 9.9% |
| Gacrux | 55 | 9.4% |

Per-item voice distribution: 85 items have **zero** audio in any voice; 424 items have audio in exactly 1 voice; 78 have audio in 2–4 voices. In an unfiltered mixed session, ~72–90% of items silently lose their play button depending on which voice got picked.

### Design decision

Take the clip from the **earliest lesson** (smallest `lessons.order_index`) that has audio for the text. Tie-breakers: `audio_clips.created_at ASC`, then `audio_clips.id` for full determinism.

Chosen because:

- Voice consistency within a session is not pedagogically meaningful for practice (exercises are atomic, not conversational).
- "Earliest lesson" matches the user's mental model — a word they first learned with voice X keeps being heard in voice X.
- Deterministic — the same word always plays in the same voice across sessions.
- No new storage cost, no generator changes.

### Why the lesson reader is untouched

`src/pages/Lesson.tsx` *does* consume `useAudio()` (line 178 in `SectionContent`, plus the `<AudioProvider>` at line 824). It genuinely needs voice-keyed lookups because:

- Dialogue lines use per-speaker voices from `lessons.dialogue_voices` (`Lesson.tsx:98`).
- Vocabulary/grammar/pronunciation items use the lesson's own `primary_voice` (`Lesson.tsx:152`).
- A single lesson can render different voices for different sections in the same render tree. A flat `Map<text, path>` would collapse them.

So this plan **does not modify `AudioContext.tsx`, `Lesson.tsx`, or the existing `fetchAudioMap` / `resolveAudioUrl`**. Practice gets a new, parallel pipeline (`SessionAudioContext`, `fetchSessionAudioMap`, `resolveSessionAudioUrl`).

### Out of scope

- **85 items with no audio in any voice.** 82 belong to Lesson 9 (`primary_voice` is `NULL`); 3 are stragglers from lessons 1 and 3. Fix separately: assign Lesson 9 a `primary_voice` and run `bun scripts/generate-exercise-audio.ts 9`. After this change, `indonesian.audio_coverage_report` (`scripts/migration.sql:1271-1288`) still correctly reports them — the operator signal survives.
- **Audio generation pipeline (`scripts/generate-exercise-audio.ts`).** Unchanged. Still generates per-lesson-voice clips and still queries the old `get_audio_clips(texts, voices)` RPC for its "already exists?" dedup check.
- **Renaming `fetchAudioMap` → `fetchLessonAudioMap`.** Tempting for symmetry with `fetchSessionAudioMap`, but deferred to keep this PR focused.

---

## Data model

Two coexisting, non-overlapping audio lookup APIs:

| API | Used by | Shape | RPC |
|---|---|---|---|
| `fetchAudioMap(texts, voiceIds)` → `Map<voice, Map<text, path>>` | Lesson reader (`src/pages/Lesson.tsx`) | Voice-keyed | `get_audio_clips(text[], text[])` (existing) |
| `fetchSessionAudioMap(texts)` → `Map<text, path>` | Practice (`src/pages/Session.tsx` + exercises) | Text-keyed | `get_audio_clip_per_text(text[])` (new) |

Correspondingly, two React contexts:

| Context | Provider | Consumer |
|---|---|---|
| `AudioContext` (existing, untouched) | `<AudioProvider>` in `Lesson.tsx` | `SectionContent` in `Lesson.tsx` |
| `SessionAudioContext` (new) | `<SessionAudioProvider>` in `Session.tsx` | 10 exercise components |

The audio generation script continues to use `get_audio_clips(texts, voiceIds)` for its per-voice dedup check — the new RPC is read-only and purpose-built for the client's earliest-lesson resolution.

---

## Supabase Requirements

### Schema changes

Append to `scripts/migration.sql` (idempotent, re-runnable):

```sql
-- Session audio resolution: return one clip per normalized text, preferring the
-- clip generated for the lesson with the smallest order_index. Ties broken by
-- created_at then id for determinism. Clips whose generated_for_lesson_id is
-- NULL (orphaned via ON DELETE SET NULL) sort last via NULLS LAST — they're
-- still usable as a last-resort fallback rather than excluded.
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION indonesian.get_audio_clip_per_text(p_texts text[])
RETURNS TABLE(normalized_text text, storage_path text)
LANGUAGE sql STABLE SET search_path = indonesian AS $$
  SELECT DISTINCT ON (ac.normalized_text)
    ac.normalized_text, ac.storage_path
  FROM audio_clips ac
  LEFT JOIN lessons l ON l.id = ac.generated_for_lesson_id
  WHERE ac.normalized_text = ANY(p_texts)
  ORDER BY ac.normalized_text, l.order_index NULLS LAST, ac.created_at, ac.id;
$$;

GRANT EXECUTE ON FUNCTION indonesian.get_audio_clip_per_text(text[]) TO authenticated;
```

RLS: none needed — `audio_clips` already grants `SELECT` to `authenticated` and has a permissive read policy (`migration.sql:1212-1219`).

### homelab-configs changes

- [ ] PostgREST: N/A — `indonesian` schema is already exposed.
- [ ] Kong: N/A — same host, same CORS config.
- [ ] GoTrue: N/A.
- [ ] Storage: N/A — reuses `indonesian-tts` bucket.

### Health check additions

Not required. `indonesian.audio_coverage_report` (`migration.sql:1271-1288`) already reports items with no audio in any voice, which remains the correct operator signal for Lesson 9 and any future uncovered content.

## Tasks

### Task 1: Add `get_audio_clip_per_text` RPC

**Files:**
- Modify: `scripts/migration.sql` (append after the existing `get_audio_clips` definition around line 1237)

**Step 1: Append the migration**

Append to `scripts/migration.sql`:

```sql
-- Session audio resolution: one clip per text, preferring earliest lesson.
-- See docs/plans/2026-04-21-session-audio-voice-resolution.md for rationale.
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION indonesian.get_audio_clip_per_text(p_texts text[])
RETURNS TABLE(normalized_text text, storage_path text)
LANGUAGE sql STABLE SET search_path = indonesian AS $$
  SELECT DISTINCT ON (ac.normalized_text)
    ac.normalized_text, ac.storage_path
  FROM audio_clips ac
  LEFT JOIN lessons l ON l.id = ac.generated_for_lesson_id
  WHERE ac.normalized_text = ANY(p_texts)
  ORDER BY ac.normalized_text, l.order_index NULLS LAST, ac.created_at, ac.id;
$$;

GRANT EXECUTE ON FUNCTION indonesian.get_audio_clip_per_text(text[]) TO authenticated;
```

**Step 2: Apply the migration**

```bash
make migrate
```

Expected output: `Migration applied successfully.` followed by `Reloading PostgREST schema cache... Done.`

**Step 3: Verify via direct SQL**

Run against the live DB (via OpenBrain MCP or `psql`):

```sql
SELECT * FROM indonesian.get_audio_clip_per_text(ARRAY['di', 'ini', 'itu']);
```

Expected: `di` resolves to a path under `tts/despina/` (lesson 1, its primary voice), `ini` to `tts/achird/` (lesson 2), `itu` to `tts/despina/` (lesson 1). One row per input text.

**Step 4: Commit**

```bash
git add scripts/migration.sql
git commit -m "feat(audio): add get_audio_clip_per_text RPC for earliest-lesson resolution"
```

### Task 2: Add `fetchSessionAudioMap` + `resolveSessionAudioUrl`

**Files:**
- Modify: `src/services/audioService.ts`
- Modify: `src/__tests__/audioService.test.ts`

**Step 1: Write the failing tests**

Append to `src/__tests__/audioService.test.ts`:

```typescript
describe('fetchSessionAudioMap', () => {
  beforeEach(() => {
    vi.resetModules()
  })

  it('maps normalized text to storage path for each returned clip', async () => {
    vi.mocked(supabase.schema).mockReturnValue({
      rpc: vi.fn().mockResolvedValue({
        data: [
          { normalized_text: 'di', storage_path: 'tts/despina/di-abc123.mp3' },
          { normalized_text: 'ini', storage_path: 'tts/achird/ini-def456.mp3' },
        ],
        error: null,
      }),
    } as any)

    const { fetchSessionAudioMap } = await import('@/services/audioService')
    const map = await fetchSessionAudioMap(['di', 'ini'])

    expect(map.get('di')).toBe('tts/despina/di-abc123.mp3')
    expect(map.get('ini')).toBe('tts/achird/ini-def456.mp3')
  })

  it('returns empty map for empty inputs', async () => {
    const { fetchSessionAudioMap } = await import('@/services/audioService')
    const map = await fetchSessionAudioMap([])
    expect(map.size).toBe(0)
  })

  it('returns empty map when RPC errors (silent — audio is non-fatal)', async () => {
    vi.mocked(supabase.schema).mockReturnValue({
      rpc: vi.fn().mockResolvedValue({ data: null, error: new Error('boom') }),
    } as any)

    const { fetchSessionAudioMap } = await import('@/services/audioService')
    const map = await fetchSessionAudioMap(['x'])
    expect(map.size).toBe(0)
  })

  it('normalizes input texts before sending to the RPC', async () => {
    const rpc = vi.fn().mockResolvedValue({ data: [], error: null })
    vi.mocked(supabase.schema).mockReturnValue({ rpc } as any)

    const { fetchSessionAudioMap } = await import('@/services/audioService')
    await fetchSessionAudioMap(['  Apa?  ', 'INI'])

    expect(rpc).toHaveBeenCalledWith('get_audio_clip_per_text', { p_texts: ['apa?', 'ini'] })
  })
})

describe('resolveSessionAudioUrl', () => {
  it('returns the public URL for a matching normalized text', async () => {
    const { resolveSessionAudioUrl } = await import('@/services/audioService')
    const map = new Map([['batik', 'tts/despina/batik-abc.mp3']])
    const url = resolveSessionAudioUrl(map, 'Batik')
    expect(url).toContain('/storage/v1/object/public/indonesian-tts/tts/despina/batik-abc.mp3')
  })

  it('returns undefined when no clip matches', async () => {
    const { resolveSessionAudioUrl } = await import('@/services/audioService')
    const map = new Map([['batik', 'tts/despina/batik-abc.mp3']])
    expect(resolveSessionAudioUrl(map, 'halus')).toBeUndefined()
  })
})
```

Note: the RPC-error test asserts silent behaviour (no log, empty map) to match the existing `fetchAudioMap` contract. Audio is non-fatal — the session continues without it; no need to noise up `error_logs`.

**Step 2: Run the failing tests**

```bash
bun run test audioService
```

Expected: new tests fail — functions not exported. Legacy `fetchAudioMap` tests still pass.

**Step 3: Implement the new exports**

Append to `src/services/audioService.ts` (keep existing `fetchAudioMap` / `resolveAudioUrl` untouched):

```typescript
export type SessionAudioMap = Map<string, string> // normalized_text → storage_path

export async function fetchSessionAudioMap(texts: string[]): Promise<SessionAudioMap> {
  if (texts.length === 0) return new Map()

  const normalized = [...new Set(texts.map(normalizeTtsText))]

  const { data, error } = await supabase
    .schema('indonesian')
    .rpc('get_audio_clip_per_text', { p_texts: normalized })

  if (error || !data) return new Map()

  const map: SessionAudioMap = new Map()
  for (const clip of data as Array<{ normalized_text: string; storage_path: string }>) {
    map.set(clip.normalized_text, clip.storage_path)
  }
  return map
}

export function resolveSessionAudioUrl(map: SessionAudioMap, text: string): string | undefined {
  const path = map.get(normalizeTtsText(text))
  return path ? `${SUPABASE_URL}/storage/v1/object/public/indonesian-tts/${path}` : undefined
}
```

**Step 4: Run tests**

```bash
bun run test audioService
```

Expected: all audioService tests pass.

**Step 5: Commit**

```bash
git add src/services/audioService.ts src/__tests__/audioService.test.ts
git commit -m "feat(audio): add voice-agnostic session audio map helpers"
```

### Task 3: Create `SessionAudioContext` (new file — `AudioContext` left untouched)

**Files:**
- Create: `src/contexts/SessionAudioContext.tsx`

**Context:** A dedicated context for practice keeps the lesson reader's voice-keyed `AudioContext` completely independent. Do **not** modify `src/contexts/AudioContext.tsx` — `Lesson.tsx:19,178,824` depends on its current shape.

**Step 1: Create the new file**

`src/contexts/SessionAudioContext.tsx`:

```typescript
import { createContext, useContext } from 'react'
import type { SessionAudioMap } from '@/services/audioService'

interface SessionAudioContextValue {
  audioMap: SessionAudioMap
}

const SessionAudioContext = createContext<SessionAudioContextValue>({
  audioMap: new Map(),
})

export function SessionAudioProvider({
  audioMap,
  children,
}: SessionAudioContextValue & { children: React.ReactNode }) {
  return (
    <SessionAudioContext.Provider value={{ audioMap }}>
      {children}
    </SessionAudioContext.Provider>
  )
}

export function useSessionAudio(): SessionAudioContextValue {
  return useContext(SessionAudioContext)
}
```

**Step 2: Commit**

```bash
git add src/contexts/SessionAudioContext.tsx
git commit -m "feat(audio): add SessionAudioContext for practice (voice-agnostic)"
```

**Step 4: Replace the pre-queue audio block**

Lines 217–250 (the full "Pre-fetch audio…" block): replace with:

```typescript
// Pre-fetch audio for all word/phrase base_texts so listening_mcq can be
// considered during queue-build. Voice-agnostic: each text resolves to the
// clip from the earliest lesson that has audio for it.
let preQueueAudioMap: SessionAudioMap = new Map()
try {
  const wordPhraseTexts = new Set<string>()
  for (const item of items) {
    if (item.item_type === 'word' || item.item_type === 'phrase') {
      wordPhraseTexts.add(normalizeTtsText(item.base_text))
    }
  }
  if (wordPhraseTexts.size > 0) {
    preQueueAudioMap = await fetchSessionAudioMap([...wordPhraseTexts])
  }
} catch {
  // Non-fatal: without pre-queue audio, listening_mcq simply won't surface
  // this session. Everything else continues.
}
```

**Step 5: Drop `voiceId` from `SessionBuildInput`**

Lines 253–272 — in the `input: SessionBuildInput = { ... }` object, delete the line `voiceId: preQueueVoiceId,`. Keep `audioMap: preQueueAudioMap`.

**Step 6: Replace the post-queue audio block**

Lines 316–364 (the whole `try { … } catch { … }` block that resolves the session voice and fetches the post-queue map): replace with:

```typescript
// Fetch audio for the final session queue (non-blocking — audio degrades
// gracefully if absent).
try {
  const textsSet = new Set<string>()
  for (const qItem of shapedQueue) {
    const item = qItem.exerciseItem
    if (item.learningItem?.base_text) textsSet.add(normalizeTtsText(item.learningItem.base_text))
    if (item.contrastPairData) {
      item.contrastPairData.options.forEach(o => textsSet.add(normalizeTtsText(o)))
    }
    if (item.clozeMcqData) {
      const filled = item.clozeMcqData.sentence.replace('___', item.clozeMcqData.correctOptionId)
      textsSet.add(normalizeTtsText(filled))
    }
    if (item.sentenceTransformationData) {
      textsSet.add(normalizeTtsText(item.sentenceTransformationData.sourceSentence))
    }
    if (item.constrainedTranslationData) {
      item.constrainedTranslationData.acceptableAnswers.forEach(a => textsSet.add(normalizeTtsText(a)))
    }
    if (item.cuedRecallData) {
      textsSet.add(normalizeTtsText(item.cuedRecallData.correctOptionId))
    }
  }
  if (textsSet.size > 0) {
    const map = await fetchSessionAudioMap([...textsSet])
    setAudioMap(map)
  }
} catch {
  // Non-fatal: audio is purely additive, session continues without it
}
```

**Step 7: Swap the provider**

Line 526 — replace:
```tsx
<AudioProvider audioMap={audioMap} voiceId={sessionVoiceId}>
```
with:
```tsx
<SessionAudioProvider audioMap={audioMap}>
```
and the matching closing tag.

`src/__tests__/sessionFlow.test.tsx:95` currently mocks `getLessonsWithVoice`. Since Session.tsx no longer calls it, replace/remove that mock line. The surviving mock is:

Note: the existing supabase mock in this test file does not mock `rpc()` — the session's `fetchSessionAudioMap` call will throw, but Session.tsx's try/catch swallows it silently (same as today with `fetchAudioMap`). No other test changes required here.

**Step 1: Per-component edits**

In each of the 10 components, apply three edits:

1. **Import of resolver** — change:
   ```typescript
   import { resolveAudioUrl } from '@/services/audioService'
   ```
   to:
   ```typescript
   import { resolveSessionAudioUrl } from '@/services/audioService'
   ```

2. **Import of hook** — change:
   ```typescript
   import { useAudio } from '@/contexts/AudioContext'
   ```
   to:
   ```typescript
   import { useSessionAudio } from '@/contexts/SessionAudioContext'
   ```

3. **Hook destructure** — change:
   ```typescript
   const { audioMap, voiceId } = useAudio()
   ```
   to:
   ```typescript
   const { audioMap } = useSessionAudio()
   ```

4. **Every call site** — strip `voiceId &&` / `voiceId ?` from the guard and swap the function call. **Preserve any other surrounding conditions** (e.g. `isAnswered`). Examples:
   ```typescript
   // simple case
   voiceId ? resolveAudioUrl(audioMap, TEXT, voiceId) : undefined
   //  becomes
   resolveSessionAudioUrl(audioMap, TEXT)

   // compound guard — keep the non-voiceId condition
   isAnswered && voiceId ? resolveAudioUrl(audioMap, TEXT, voiceId) : undefined
   //  becomes
   isAnswered ? resolveSessionAudioUrl(audioMap, TEXT) : undefined
   ```
   Compound guards appear in `ClozeMcq.tsx:139`, `ConstrainedTranslationExercise.tsx:189,278`, `SentenceTransformationExercise.tsx:85`, `CuedRecallExercise.tsx:67`.

Exact lines per file:

| File | Destructure line | Call-site lines |
|---|---:|---|
| `ListeningMCQ.tsx` | 22 | 23 |
| `Dictation.tsx` | 20 | 21 |
| `RecognitionMCQ.tsx` | 23 | 25 |
| `MeaningRecall.tsx` | 21 | 74 |
| `TypedRecall.tsx` | 20 | 115 |
| `CuedRecallExercise.tsx` | 20 | 67 |
| `ClozeMcq.tsx` | 22 | 139 |
| `ContrastPairExercise.tsx` | 22 | 141 |
| `SentenceTransformationExercise.tsx` | 83 | 85 |
| `ConstrainedTranslationExercise.tsx` | 28 | 189, 278 |

After edits, verify no stale references remain:

```bash
grep -rn "useAudio\|voiceId" src/components/exercises/
```

Expected output: no matches.

**Step 2: Update the two affected tests**

`src/__tests__/listeningMcqExercise.test.tsx` and `src/__tests__/dictationExercise.test.tsx`:

- `import { AudioProvider } from '@/contexts/AudioContext'` → `import { SessionAudioProvider } from '@/contexts/SessionAudioContext'`.
- Every `<AudioProvider audioMap={...} voiceId={...}>` → `<SessionAudioProvider audioMap={...}>`.
- Every `Map<voice, Map<text, path>>` fixture flattens to `Map<text, path>`.

Example:

```tsx
// old
<AudioProvider audioMap={new Map([['voice', new Map([['x', 'p']])]])} voiceId="voice">
// new
<SessionAudioProvider audioMap={new Map([['x', 'p']])}>
```

**Step 3: Run the full test suite**

```bash
bun run test
```

Expected: all passing.

**Step 4: Type-check and build**

```bash
bun run build
```

Expected: type-check passes, Vite build succeeds.

**Step 5: Lint**

```bash
bun run lint
```

Expected: no new warnings.

**Step 6: Manual verification per CLAUDE.md**

```bash
bun run dev
```

In browser:

1. Log in, start an unfiltered practice session from the dashboard.
2. Check play-button visibility across ~10 exercises. Audio should be present for essentially every word/phrase item with a clip in *any* voice (502/587 now — the missing 85 are the Lesson-9 + stragglers residual).
3. Start a lesson-filtered session. Play buttons should still work.
4. Open a lesson → navigate to a dialogue section. Verify speaker-specific voices still render (sanity check that this plan's "don't touch Lesson.tsx" claim holds).

**Step 7: Commit**

```bash
git add -A
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat(audio): exercises consume SessionAudioContext, play buttons render for all items

Completes the migration of practice session audio to the voice-agnostic
SessionAudioContext. Each of the 10 exercise components now resolves audio
against a flat Map<text, path> populated by get_audio_clip_per_text — the
clip from the earliest lesson that has audio for the text.

Fixes ~72-90% of word/phrase items silently losing their play button in
unfiltered practice sessions. Lesson reader (Lesson.tsx) unchanged — it
still uses voice-keyed AudioContext for dialogue speakers and per-section
voice variation.

Residual: 85 items still have no audio in any voice (82 in Lesson 9 with
NULL primary_voice). Fix separately by assigning Lesson 9 a voice and
running `bun scripts/generate-exercise-audio.ts 9`.
EOF
)"
```

---

### Task 7: Post-deploy verification

**Files:** none

**Step 1: Deploy per CLAUDE.md**

Push to `main`, let GitHub Actions build, pull the image on the homelab, recreate the container (the standard flow documented in CLAUDE.md > Deployment).

**Step 2: Browser verification**

1. Open `indonesian.duin.home/session` with no lesson filter.
2. In the Network panel, filter for `indonesian-tts`. Observe a spread of voice paths (`tts/despina/…`, `tts/achird/…`, `tts/algenib/…`, etc.) rather than just one.
3. Pick 5 items from the queue at random. For each word/phrase item whose text has at least one clip in `audio_clips`, the play button renders and plays on click.

**Step 3: Coverage evidence**

Read-only sanity query:

```sql
WITH item_texts AS (
  SELECT DISTINCT li.id, li.normalized_text
  FROM indonesian.learning_items li
  WHERE li.item_type IN ('word', 'phrase')
)
SELECT
  COUNT(*) AS total,
  COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE EXISTS (
    SELECT 1 FROM indonesian.audio_clips ac WHERE ac.normalized_text = it.normalized_text
  )) AS items_now_playable
FROM item_texts it;
```

Expected: `items_now_playable = 502 / 587` (85%), compared to the old ceiling of ~27.6% in the best single-voice session.

---

## Testing summary

| Layer | Test file | What it covers |
|---|---|---|
| Service | `src/__tests__/audioService.test.ts` | `fetchSessionAudioMap` + `resolveSessionAudioUrl` behaviour; legacy `fetchAudioMap` still works |
| Queue logic | `src/__tests__/sessionQueue.test.ts` | `hasAudioFor` uses the flat map; `listening_mcq` eligibility gates correctly |
| Exercise UX | `src/__tests__/listeningMcqExercise.test.tsx`, `src/__tests__/dictationExercise.test.tsx` | Components render audio UI with the new context + map shape |
| Session init | `src/__tests__/sessionFlow.test.tsx` | Updated mock (drop `getLessonsWithVoice`); session builds without voice resolution |
| Manual | — | Unfiltered session shows play buttons broadly; lesson reader dialogue still uses per-speaker voices |

---

## Rollback plan

If the new RPC causes any issues in production:

1. `DROP FUNCTION indonesian.get_audio_clip_per_text(text[]);` — safe, no data. The RPC is read-only and purpose-built; nothing else depends on it.
2. Revert the client commits — the lesson reader's `AudioContext` and `fetchAudioMap` code paths are untouched and fully functional. Practice falls back to the old one-voice behaviour (which is still broken in the way this plan fixes, but not *more* broken than before).

No data migration; no destructive schema change; fully reversible.
